bio.name: Faizan Alam
bio.intro: Mechatronics Engineering graduate with multidisciplinary expertise in robotics, AI, computer vision, embedded systems, and software engineering. Proven experience architecting and deploying intelligent robotic systems by integrating mechanical design, perception algorithms, control systems, and full-stack software into cohesive solutions. Skilled in ROS, Python, C++, TensorFlow, OpenCV, CAD, and system integration, with a strong foundation in autonomous robotics, machine learning, and hardware-software co-design. Dedicated to developing high-performance engineering solutions that bridge advanced research with practical deployment.

timeline.title: VEX IQ Robotics Coach
bullets: Coached students through full engineering design process from concept to competition-ready prototype
Delivered live instruction on hardware/software integration, mechanics, and programming fundamentals

timeline.title: Robotics Curriculum Developer
bullets: Designed 3D CAD models of mechanical systems including gearboxes, bar lifts, and drivetrains in OnShape
Built and tested example robots as teaching aids, validating designs through iterative physical testing

timeline.title: AI Data Trainer & Evaluator
bullets: Evaluated LLM outputs against structured test suites; contributed to 50% improvement in model response relevancy

timeline.title: Lead Robotics Engineer & Manager
bullets: Built TensorFlow and OpenCV detection pipelines in Java achieving 100% increase in mechanism speed
Coached 1 FLL and 2 FTC teams to 2x 1st and 1x 2nd place at qualifiers; top 10/20 provincially

timeline.title: Programming & Robotics Developer
bullets: Engineered 30+ coding projects as training material; developed HTML/CSS lessons improving engagement by 35%
Mentored 50+ students in Java, Arduino, Python — covering OOP, PID control, sensors, and multi-subsystem architectures

projects.title: Hand Gesture–Controlled Stewart Platform
projects.summary: 6-DOF Stewart platform with real-time hand gesture recognition at 98% accuracy.
projects.challenge: Build a 6-DOF motion control system using natural hand gestures — without commercial motion capture hardware and within a tight budget.
projects.approach: Implemented real-time gesture recognition using Python and MediaPipe, mapping 6 distinct gestures to 6 degrees of freedom. Designed and iterated servo-driven parallel mechanism in Arduino. Designed all mechanical supports in OnShape.
projects.outcome: 98% gesture recognition accuracy across 6 DOF. Delivered within 50% of budget. 100+ participants operated the prototype at the final exhibition.

projects.title: Mobile Robotics — ROS / LiDAR
projects.summary: ROS-based TurtleBot for autonomous navigation, object detection, and LiDAR path planning.
projects.challenge: Implement autonomous navigation, colour recognition, and object detection on a mobile robot platform using ROS on Linux.
projects.approach: Co-developed ROS-based TurtleBot implementing path planning, trajectory following, colour and object recognition. Scripted LiDAR-based navigation simulations for a skid-steer vehicle separately.
projects.outcome: Completed all milestone demos in under 3 minutes. Ranked in the top 10% of the class.

projects.title: PEIE STEM Camp Portal
projects.summary: Full-stack camp management portal with key-gated sections for eah class and Google Sheets attendance that auto checks in students after they are signed in..
projects.challenge: It was challenging to deliver lessons because the presentation slides could not be shown on a shared screen and the presentation files were not accessible to student devices.
projects.approach: Developed a fully static web app, serverless, with data storage via the GitHub Contents API, student and admin portals gated by keys for access control, and an attendance system built on Google Sheets/Forms and automated via Google Apps Script.
projects.outcome: Delivered a user-friendly web application that streamlined attendance tracking, lesson presentation access, and camp administration within a single platform.

projects.title: Work Flow Assist PWA
projects.summary: Personal productivity PWA with priority engine, task estimator, and daily planner.
projects.challenge: Create a client-side productivity tool that works offline and persists data without any backend infrastructure.
projects.approach: Built a PWA with Eisenhower-style priority engine, guided task estimator, and a Daily Assistant planner with Focus and Spread modes using localStorage persistence.
projects.outcome: Fully functional offline-capable PWA. Used daily for personal task management.
